title: Hook manifest
description: Registers a hook with Atelier's hook system (§15). Hooks fire at named lifecycle events around tool dispatch and verification. Manifest files live in `~/.atelier/hooks/` (global) or `<repo>/.atelier/hooks/` (per-repo, overrides global by name). First-use approval flows through §8 trust budget. Over-budget hooks emit a warning and continue — never block the turn.

name.description: Hook identifier surfaced in the §8 trust budget UI and in any over-budget warnings.

event.description: Lifecycle event this hook responds to. See §15 for semantics.

tool_filter.description: If present, hook fires only for tool calls whose tool name matches one of these glob patterns. Default: fires for all tools. Ignored for on-verify-* events.

implementation.description: Hook impls deliberately do NOT support implementation-level `timeout_ms` — hooks have manifest-level `time_budget_ms` with warn-but-never-block semantics (§15). Kept inline (rather than $ref'ing config/_implementation.v1.json) so tool-level `timeout_ms` cannot leak in via the shared schema. v1 supports shell hooks only; HTTP hook manifests are reserved until a production HTTP executor exists.

env.description: Environment overrides. Values support ${env:NAME}; ${keychain:NAME} is reserved and fails closed until OS keychain support lands. v60.37 SCH-M4 — values capped at 4 KiB.

time_budget_ms.description: Median budget for this hook's execution. Over-budget hooks emit a warning panel in the UI and the turn proceeds — they never block (§15).

allow_net.description: Shell-only: permit outbound network from the subprocess sandbox.
